title: F5 BIG-IP Advanced Firewall Manager (AFM) Deny and Drop Events
dataSources: F5 BIG-IP AFM via Telemetry Streaming or syslog, commonly `sourcetype=f5:bigip:afm` within `f5:telemetry:json` or dedicated AFM sourcetype per TA version
app: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP (`Splunk_TA_f5-bigip`, Splunkbase 2680)
spl:
index=network (sourcetype="f5:bigip:afm" OR (sourcetype="f5:telemetry:json" source="*afm*"))
| eval act=lower(coalesce(action, disposition, verdict))
| where match(act,"(?i)drop|deny|reject|block")
| stats count by src, dest, dest_port, rule_name, host
| sort -count
| head 50
description: AFM network firewall denies show attempted east-west or north-south connections that violate stateful inspection, IP intelligence, or explicit ACL policies. Concentrated denies from single sources may indicate scanning, lateral movement, or misconfigured peers.
value: Provides network-layer detection complementary to ASM and edge firewalls, with context on which BIG-IP enforced the deny and which rule fired.
implementation: Enable AFM logging through Telemetry Streaming or syslog as supported by your BIG-IP version and TA release. Confirm whether events arrive under `f5:bigip:afm` or nested `f5:telemetry:json` and adjust the search filter. Normalize `action` and port fields; alert on denies toward management or database segments.
visualization: Map (source countries), Table (top denies by rule), Time chart (deny rate).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP — Telemetry and AFM
Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP
